description_long: This object is a vintage wooden television set, likely from the mid-20th century, featuring a rectangular boxy form with a polished wood veneer exterior. The front panel displays a convex glass screen bordered by a thick wooden bezel, with three vertical decorative grille sections on each side, possibly for speakers or ventilation. Below the screen are four round control knobs, likely for power, volume, tuning, and other adjustments, all finished in a matching brown tone. The left and right sides are plain, with the right side featuring a circular port, possibly for ventilation or an antenna connection. The back panel is open, revealing internal electronic components such as vacuum tubes, transformers, and wiring, all mounted on a metal chassis. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with a focus on durability and function typical of early television sets.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, decorative front grilles, and exposed rear electronics. The design is boxy, robust, and features four control knobs below the screen.
description_view_0: The front view shows the convex glass screen, three vertical grille sections on each side, and four round control knobs below the screen.
description_view_1: The right side view is plain except for a circular port, likely for ventilation or antenna connection.
description_view_2: The rear view exposes the internal electronic components, including vacuum tubes and wiring, mounted on a metal chassis.
description_view_3: The left side view is entirely plain, showing only the wood veneer surface.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick wooden bezel, brown color, with vertical decorative grilles on each side
attribute_control_button_layout: Four round knobs in a horizontal row below the screen on the front panel

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
